message:
feat: support leading-dimension expansion in tensor_repeat (PyTorch-compatible)

PyTorch's Tensor.repeat() allows the repeats tuple to be longer than
the input rank, implicitly prepending size-1 dimensions.  Previously
our implementation required exact rank equality and rejected any
mismatch.

ft-autograd/src/lib.rs:
- Add normalize_repeat_shape() helper: when repeats.len() > rank,
  prepend leading 1-dims; when repeats.len() < rank, fail closed with
  ShapeMismatch
- Forward pass: use normalized shape for strides and source indexing
  so that a 1-D [3] tensor with repeats=[2,3] produces a [2,9] output
- Backward pass: same normalization so gradient accumulation maps
  correctly back to the original (un-expanded) shape

ft-api/src/lib.rs:
- Update tensor_repeat doc to describe the new semantics
- Replace session_repeat_rejects_rank_mismatch with
  session_repeat_with_leading_dims: [2]-tensor repeated by [2,3]
  yields shape [2,6] with correct tiled values
- Add session_repeat_rejects_short_repeat_tuple: repeats shorter than
  rank is still rejected
- Add session_repeat_backward_with_leading_dims: gradient of
  sum(repeat([1,2,3], [2,3])) == [6,6,6] (each element appears in
  2*3=6 output positions)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
